name: dependency-management
description: Dependency management specialist. Use when updating dependencies, scanning
capabilities: Parses dependency files from npm, pip, Poetry, and Maven
Scans for security vulnerabilities using package manager tools
Checks license compatibility for all dependencies
Identifies outdated packages and version conflicts
author: Joseph OBrien
securitySummary: Static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The analyzer misidentified markdown code blocks as Ruby/shell backtick execution (documentation examples, not code). YAML frontmatter fields were flagged as weak cryptographic algorithms (false match). The actual Python helper script only reads explicitly-passed files and outputs JSON. No network calls, no data exfiltration, no credential access. Behavior matches stated purpose.
valueStatement: Managing project dependencies manually is time-consuming and risky. Outdated dependencies create security vulnerabilities. This skill automates dependency analysis, vulnerability scanning, and license compliance checks across multiple package managers.
limitations: Does not execute updates without user confirmation
Does not access private package registries
Requires external tools for full vulnerability scanning

useCases.title: Security vulnerability scan
useCases.description: Scan Node.js or Python projects for known vulnerabilities and receive fix recommendations.

useCases.title: License compliance check
useCases.description: Verify all dependencies have compatible licenses before releasing to production.

useCases.title: Dependency health audit
useCases.description: Generate comprehensive reports on dependency age, duplicates, and maintenance status.

promptTemplates.title: Scan for vulnerabilities
promptTemplates.prompt: Scan my dependencies for security vulnerabilities and show me which packages need updates.

promptTemplates.title: Check outdated packages
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me all outdated dependencies in my project with their current and latest versions.

promptTemplates.title: License review
promptTemplates.prompt: Check license compatibility for all dependencies in my project and flag any issues.

promptTemplates.title: Generate audit report
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a complete dependency audit including vulnerabilities, outdated packages, license compliance, and recommendations.

bestPractices: Review changelogs before applying major dependency updates
Test your project after updating dependencies
Use lock files to ensure reproducible dependency versions
antiPatterns: Blindly updating all dependencies without reviewing changes
Ignoring medium and low severity vulnerability warnings
Using floating version ranges for critical dependencies

faq.question: Which package managers are supported?
faq.answer: Supports npm, pip, Poetry, Maven, Gradle, and Cargo with additional reference guides.

faq.question: What are the limits on dependency count?
faq.answer: No hard limits. Processing time scales with project size. Very large projects may take longer.

faq.question: How does it integrate with my existing tools?
faq.answer: Uses native package manager commands like npm audit and pip-audit for security scanning.

faq.question: Is my project data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only reads dependency files you specify. No data is sent to external servers.

faq.question: Why did the scan fail?
faq.answer: Common issues include missing lock files, invalid dependency syntax, or unavailable package manager tools.

faq.question: How is this different from Dependabot?
faq.answer: This skill provides interactive analysis and recommendations. Dependabot automates pull requests for updates.
